notes: Transfer students are strongly encouraged to work closely with the California Community College counselors for transfer preparation to UC Davis. Transfer students may also contact a UC Davis transfer advisor for assistance. The below agreements should be utilized in conjunction with professional advising.
Please note that the Department Agreements as published on ASSIST are also important documents and should be referenced in conjunction with the major agreements as needed.
Please refer to the Transfer Admissions Requirements for information on admissions to this major.

Guarantee your admission to UC Davis! The UC Davis Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a written agreement ensuring your admission to UC Davis from any California Community College. Please visit the TAG webpage for more information!

The A.B. degree in Theatre & Dance provides students with an appreciation for an understanding of performance and its role in culture and society. Students build skills in specific areas as well as a broad knowledge of theatre and dance. Each year's schedule includes opportunities to work with professional artists (directors, choreographers and writers) in Granada Artists-in Residence productions, faculty and student-led workshops and productions, and the Film Festival at UC Davis. These productions are staged in our proscenium (Main), thrust (Wyatt), black box (Arena), performance studio (Della Davidson Performance Studio) and intimate laboratory theatre (Lab A). These productions are part of the academic program of the department and serve an important purpose in the study of theatre and dance. Participation is open to all students. All students completing a major in Theatre & Dance must participate in productions, including work in at least two of the following three areas: performance (acting/dance), design, and directing/playwriting/stage management, as well as crew assignments and work in production labs.
